headline: GRAINS EXTEND SLIDE, SILVER FALLS SHARPLY FIVE PERCENT
subheadline: Corn hits four-week low as dollar strength overwhelms commodity complex.
lead: Corn dropped to $4.17, the lowest close since mid-May, as selling that started last Tuesday shows no signs of stopping. The dollar's 0.7% rise to $100.07 is pulling money out of everything that isn't denominated in greenbacks, and grain futures are getting hit hardest. Silver's 5.2% fall to $69.10 tells the story: when the dollar flexes, commodities fold.
the_takeaway: Dollar strength is running the commodity complex, not fundamentals.
teaser: Four-day grain slide deepens as dollar strength overwhelms the complex.

one_number.unit: cents corn lost in four sessions
one_number.context: Corn has shed 17 cents since Thursday's close, the steepest four-day decline since March. No weather premium, no export disruption, no USDA surprise. Just relentless selling as funds rotate out of commodities into dollar-denominated assets. When selling has no catalyst, it usually means position unwinding, not fundamental deterioration.

yesterdays_call.summary: Dollar strength overwhelmed industrial silver demand fundamentals.
yesterdays_call.note: Silver's 5.2% fall confirmed the dollar was in charge, not metal-specific factors.

sections.title: GRAINS EXTEND FOUR-DAY SLIDE
sections.body: Corn fell another 4½ cents to $4.17, the **fourth straight session lower** and the lowest close since mid-May. Soybeans dropped to $11.21, now sitting 77 cents off the April highs. The selling has no clean catalyst beyond dollar strength and fund liquidation. **Crop conditions remain favorable** across the Belt, rainfall adequate, and planting wrapped up ahead of schedule. This is position unwinding, not fundamental deterioration.
sections.catalyst: Dollar Index rose 0.7% to $100.07, pulling funds out of commodities
sections.bottom_line: Selling is technical, not fundamental; crops are fine.

sections.title: METALS FALL ON DOLLAR MOVE HIGHER
sections.body: Silver broke down 5.2% to $69.10, the **biggest single-day drop since February**, as dollar strength overwhelmed industrial demand. Gold fell 2.3% to $4,365, confirming this is currency-driven selling, not metal-specific weakness. The Dollar Index at $100.07 is approaching the psychological century mark that triggers algorithmic selling in commodities. **When the dollar runs, metals run for the exits first.**
sections.catalyst: Dollar Index gained 0.7% toward 100 level, triggering algorithmic commodity selling
sections.bottom_line: Dollar at 100 is the line; metals break first when it gets there.

sections.title: HOGS FALL ON SCREWWORM FEARS
sections.body: Lean hogs dropped 2.8% to $98.80 as **New World screwworm cases in Texas** prompted Canada to ban livestock imports from the state. Two calves and a dog tested positive for the flesh-eating pest, the second confirmed case in South Texas within six miles of the first. USDA is ramping up border trapping and several states are implementing temporary import restrictions. The psychological impact is outweighing the actual supply disruption so far.
sections.catalyst: Second New World screwworm case confirmed in Texas, prompting Canadian import ban
sections.bottom_line: Disease fears bigger than actual supply impact, so far.
sections.vs_yesterday: Hogs: screwworm news adds fundamental pressure to technical selling.

outside_the_pit.title: Tyson names Wes Morris COO in leadership shuffle
outside_the_pit.body: Morris will oversee all business segments including Chicken, Beef, Pork, Prepared Foods and International. Leadership changes at major processors matter because they signal strategic shifts that eventually show up in basis and contract terms.

outside_the_pit.title: Cattle cycle expansion signals remain mixed despite high calf prices
outside_the_pit.body: Heifer retention stays minimal while beef cow culling hits record lows since 2023. The cycle wants to turn but producers aren't committing yet, keeping the supply outlook uncertain for 2027-2028.

outside_the_pit.title: House passes 2027 ag appropriations with FSIS funding increase
outside_the_pit.body: $10.8M increase targets state inspection programs and frontline meat inspectors. More inspection capacity could ease processing bottlenecks that have been constraining livestock prices.

spread_to_watch.label: July corn / December corn carry
spread_to_watch.commentary: The carry isn't breaking down despite four days of selling, which means this is fund liquidation, not storage pressure. If fundamentals were deteriorating, the front month would be leading lower and the carry would blow wider. Instead, both months are falling together.

basis.headline: Eastern Corn Belt basis steady despite futures slide
basis.body: **Illinois River basis holding flat** while futures drop tells you local demand is solid. Ethanol plants are still bidding for nearby bushels even as the futures board gets hit. Western Corn Belt staying soft, consistent with the seasonal and longer haul to processors.

weekly_thread.question: Will the dollar break 100 and drag commodities into a deeper correction?
weekly_thread.status_text: Dollar Index hit $100.07 today, within touching distance of the psychological 100 level that historically triggers algorithmic commodity selling. Silver's 5.2% fall suggests the algos are already firing. This week decides whether we get a technical break or just another test.

the_more_you_know.title: Why the dollar at 100 matters for grain prices
the_more_you_know.body: The Dollar Index hit $100.07 today, approaching the century mark that triggers algorithmic selling programs in commodity futures. **When DXY crosses 100, systematic funds automatically reduce commodity exposure** because their risk models flag currency headwinds. It's not fundamental analysis, it's programmed math. The 2022 grain rally stalled when the dollar broke 110. The 2015 crop price breakdown accelerated when DXY crossed 100 and stayed there. Today's 17-cent corn decline in four days fits the pattern: systematic selling, not weather or export news.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress: corn above 90% planted removes weather premium entirely
Dollar Index: above 100.50 triggers more algorithmic commodity selling
Texas screwworm response: expanded quarantine zones would pressure hogs further

daily_quote.text: The wise man puts all his eggs in one basket and watches the basket.
